events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need assess transmitter installation. Scenario: DP impulse lines sloped wrong for service: gas taps below pipe / liquid taps on top - classic. Work rough-complete. Need decide pass/fail/needs_more_info. Since non-compliant, dangerous? If work dangerous or non-compliant, decision must fail. So fail. Risk? Wrong impulse line slope can cause measurement error, liquid lock in gas lines, gas pockets in liquid lines, leading to inaccurate control, possible process upset. Not necessarily immediate safety critical but high? For instrumentation, incorrect slope leads to erroneous readings, could cause operational hazard. Risk high maybe medium. Need component_conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete maybe 70? Value not asked 0. Sound etc 0. Findings: impulse lines incorrectly sloped; gas service impulse taps located below pipe; liquid service taps on top causing condensate/gas pocket issues. Actions: reconfigure impulse tubing with correct slope, relocate taps per service (gas top/side, liquid side/bottom), install condensate pots/seal pots if needed, verify slope toward process or drain/vent per API RP 551 and OEM manual, re-test calibration. Rationale: API RP 551 requires impulse lines be sloped to prevent liquid accumulation in gas service and gas trapping in liquid service; manufacturer manuals specify tap orientation. Current condition violates. References: API RP 551, manufacturer transmitter installation manual. Need JSON valid. Ensure no prose outside.

    "Gas service impulse taps are located below the pipe, risking liquid accumulation",
    "Liquid service impulse taps are located on top of the pipe, risking gas pocket formation",
    "Incorrect orientation will cause erroneous differential pressure measurement and poor process control"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Reconfigure impulse tubing with correct slope per API RP 551 and OEM instructions",
    "Relocate taps as required: gas service taps at top/side, liquid service taps at side/bottom",
    "Install condensate pots, seal pots, or equalizing/vent arrangements where specified",
    "Leak-test, purge, and recalibrate the transmitter after rework"
  ],
  "rationale": "API RP 551 and transmitter manufacturer installation manuals require impulse lines to be sloped and taps oriented so gas lines stay free of liquid and liquid lines stay free of gas. The observed configuration is the classic reverse arrangement, making the installation non-compliant and likely to produce inaccurate measurements.",
  "references": [
    "API RP 551 - Process Control Instrumentation",
    "Manufacturer transmitter installation manual"
  ]
